text: How each NBA team assembled their current rosters Visualized. A few months ago I found out that the Timberwolves were able to acquire the draft rights to Jaden McDaniels in part because of the 2007 trade of Kevin Garnett to the Celtics. It made me wonder, could you visualize how an NBA team assembled their entire roster just by charting out draft picks, trades, and free agency signings? This question started a months long journey of compiling the information and then making family tree style visualizations for each team.
label: analysis

text: The Thunder tree has the oldest draft pick still providing a team value. In 1977, the Supersonics drafted Jack Sikma, whose trade tree started in 1986 when he was traded to the Bucks. That trade started a tree that eventually resulted in the Thunder getting Jared McCain earlier this year.
label: analysis

text: The Celtics tree is crazy for the amount of trades. It's pretty well known that they received the pick for Jaylen Brown in the trade that sent KG and Paul Pierce to the Nets. But less known is that every player on their roster not signed in free agency or drafted with their own picks is on the Celtics partially as a result of that trade.
label: analysis

text: The Suns have a lot of trades that really didn't go their way recently and the fact that the only player on their roster there after the Beal trade being Royce O'Neale is not a fun fact. However, the KD trade is very cool to see with 5 teams involved.
label: analysis

text: Definitely not the most a team has gotten from trading Paul George but Haliburton, Siakam, Huff, Zubac, Kobe Brown, Nembahrd, Sheppard, and Furphy is still a pretty good return all things considered.
label: analysis

text: Thanks! I'm very glad I could get it out before the draft. Gotta thank Giannis for holding up all the trades long enough for me to get it presentable.
label: reaction_or_meme

text: My biggest takeaway is unfortunately Imgur sucks now. It was like 70% ads
label: reaction_or_meme

text: Furthest Draft pick is Jack Sikma, the 1977 Supersonics 1st round pick, however, he wasn't traded until July 1, 1986. The Cavaliers have them beat by a few weeks with a June 17, 1986 trade of a future 2nd for a guy by the name of Mark Price. Pretty good value.
label: analysis

text: I started with the current rosters and then used various resources like Basketball Reference, ProSportsTrades, RealGM, and random searches for some more obscure trades to check and double check how each player got to each team.
label: analysis
